items.enzyme_stability:
- RADH is unstable in buffer (half-life ~6 h at room temperature, 9% activity after 1 day at 8°C).
- Best storage: TEA–HCl buffer (10 mM, pH 7.5) at –20°C (full stability for at least 80 days).
- Lyophilized enzyme stable for 20 days at –20°C (requires reactivation).
- Addition of 50% glycerol increases initial activity to 140% after 4 days at –20°C, 120% after 80 days.
- CaCl2 (0.8 mM) stabilizes enzyme: half-life at 8°C is 163 h (with CaCl2), almost complete deactivation in 24 h without CaCl2.
- Stability optimum: pH 5.5–8, 8–15°C (half-life 130–138 h at 8–15°C with CaCl2). At 0°C, half-life is 41 h. At 25°C, half-life is 80 h.
items.inhibition_type: Substrate inhibition observed for (R)-2-HPP. NADP+ shows slight surplus inhibition at >0.8 mM.
items.maximum_reaction_rate_unit: U mg-1 (for (R)-2-HPP reduction, see Table V)
items.michaelis_constant_unit: mmol L-1 (for (R)-2-HPP reduction, see Table V)
items.special_treatment:
- All kinetic parameters determined at 30°C, in triplicate, using spectrophotometric assays at 340 nm.
- Buffers: TEA–HCl (50 mM, pH 7.5) + CaCl2 (0.8 mM) for reduction; TRIS–HCl (50 mM, pH 9.0) + CaCl2 (0.8 mM) for oxidation.
- Substrate and cofactor concentrations varied as indicated in Table V.
- Substrate range: RADH prefers aromatic/cyclic substrates, especially alpha-hydroxy ketones (see Tables II–IV).
- Stereoselectivity: Reductions catalyzed with >99% enantio- and diastereomeric excess for alpha-hydroxy ketones.
- pH optimum: Reduction pH 6–9.5 (broad), oxidation pH 10–11.5 (sharp).
- Temperature optimum: Activity increases linearly from 15 to 45°C; stability optimum 8–15°C.
- Native molecular weight: 75 kDa (trimeric structure suggested).
- No activating metal ion required for activity; Ca2+ required for stability.

items.maximum_reaction_rate_unit: U mg-1 (for benzaldehyde reduction, Table V, entry 6)
items.michaelis_constant_unit: mmol L-1 (for benzaldehyde reduction, Table V, entry 6)
items.special_treatment: measured at 30°C, in triplicate, using spectrophotometric assays at 340 nm.
